documentation.description:
Type Vectors.Vector represents an array of Boolean, each of which
takes 8 bits of the representation, with the 7 msb set to zero. Express
in contracts the constraint on valid vectors and the model that they
represent, and the relationship between input models and output model.

simple_types.name: Vector_Element
simple_types.code: type Vector_Element is mod 2 ** System.Storage_Unit with Ghost;

array_types.name: Vector_Boolean_Array
array_types.code:
type Vector_Boolean_Array is array (1 .. Vector_Boolean_Size) of Boolean
with Ghost;

constants.name: Vector_Boolean_Size

subprograms.name: Element
subprograms.code: function Element (V : Vectors.Vector; N : Positive) return Vector_Element

documentation.description: Return the Nth element represented by the vector
The model of a valid vector is the corresponding array of Boolean values

subprograms.name: Nand
subprograms.code: function Nand (Left, Right : Boolean) return Boolean

subprograms.name: Nand
subprograms.code: function Nand (Left, Right : Vectors.Vector) return Vectors.Vector

subprograms.name: Nor
subprograms.code: function Nor  (Left, Right : Boolean) return Boolean

subprograms.name: Nor
subprograms.code: function Nor (Left, Right : Vectors.Vector) return Vectors.Vector

subprograms.name: Nxor
subprograms.code: function Nxor (Left, Right : Boolean) return Boolean

subprograms.name: Nxor
subprograms.code: function Nxor (Left, Right : Vectors.Vector) return Vectors.Vector

documentation.description:
The three boolean operations "nand", "nor" and "nxor" are needed
for cases where the compiler moves boolean array operations into
the body of the loop that iterates over the array elements.

Note the following equivalences:
  (not X) or  (not Y)  =  not (X and Y)  =  Nand (X, Y)
  (not X) and (not Y)  =  not (X or Y)   =  Nor  (X, Y)
  (not X) xor (not Y)  =  X xor Y
  X       xor (not Y)  =  not (X xor Y)  =  Nxor (X, Y)

subprograms.name: Shift_Right
subprograms.code: function Shift_Right (V : Vectors.Vector; N : Natural) return Vectors.Vector

documentation.description:
A valid vector is one for which all elements are 0 (representing False)
or 1 (representing True).
